text: 2. The user starts an on-device coding agent running a user-started mobile development task
Give the on-device agent a real task
Lock or leave the app while the task continues
Yaver keeps a foreground notification while work is running
3. The task is doing real work — this can take minutes
4. A foreground notification shows it running — Android keeps the process alive
5. We leave the app. Without a foreground service Android would kill this mid-task and lose the work
6. The task keeps running in the background and finishes
[step error: timed out waiting for "Task finished"]
7. A “task finished” notification confirms the work completed while backgrounded
8. The user can stop the agent anytime — the service and notification end

[step error: local: exit status 255: Stopping service: Intent { cmp=io.yaver.mobile/.sandbox.SandboxService }
Service stopped]
